back_translation: Solomon ruled over all the kingdoms from the Euphrates River to the land of the Philistines and to the border of Egypt; those kingdoms brought tribute and served Solomon all the days of his life.
Solomon's provisions for one day were thirty cors of fine flour and sixty cors of meal,
ten fat oxen, twenty pasture-fed oxen, a hundred sheep, besides deer, gazelles, roebucks, and fattened fowl.
For he ruled over all the region west of the River, from Tiphsah to Gaza, over all the kings west of the River, and he had peace on every side.
Judah and Israel dwelt securely, each under his own vine and fig tree, from Dan to Beersheba, throughout the days of Solomon.
Solomon had forty thousand stalls for his chariot horses, and twelve thousand horsemen.
Those governors provided food for King Solomon and for all who came to King Solomon's table, each in his month; they let nothing be lacking.
And the barley and straw for the horses and swift steeds they brought to the place where it was needed, each according to his charge.
God gave Solomon wisdom and very great understanding, and breadth of mind like the sand on the seashore.
Solomon's wisdom surpassed the wisdom of all the people of the East and all the wisdom of Egypt.
He was wiser than all men—than Ethan the Ezrahite, Heman, Calcol, and Darda, the sons of Mahol—and his name spread among all the surrounding nations.
He spoke three thousand proverbs, and his songs numbered a thousand and five.
He spoke of trees, from the cedar in Lebanon to the hyssop that grows out of the wall; he spoke of beasts, birds, creeping things, and fish.
People from all nations came to hear the wisdom of Solomon, from all the kings of the earth who had heard of his wisdom.
Hiram king of Tyre sent his servants to Solomon, because he heard that Solomon had been anointed king in place of his father; for Hiram had always loved David.
And Solomon sent a message to Hiram, saying:
“You know that David my father could not build a house for the Name of the LORD his God because of the wars that surrounded him, until the LORD subdued those enemies under his feet.
But now the LORD my God has given me rest on every side; there is no adversary or evil occurrence.
Behold, I intend to build a house for the Name of the LORD my God, as the LORD said to David my father: ‘Your son whom I will set on your throne in your place, he shall build the house for my Name.’

Now therefore command that they cut for me cedars of Lebanon; my servants will work with your servants, and I will pay the wages of your servants as you set, for you know that there is no one among us skilled at cutting timber like the Sidonians.”
When Hiram heard the words of Solomon, he rejoiced greatly and said, “Blessed be the LORD this day, who has given David a wise son over this great people.”
Hiram sent word to Solomon, saying, “I have received the message you sent me; I will do all you desire regarding the cedar and cypress timber.

My servants will bring the timber down from Lebanon to the sea, and I will float it as rafts by sea to the place you specify; I will have it broken up there, and you will carry it away. And you shall do what I desire by providing food for my household.”
So Hiram supplied Solomon with all the cedar and cypress timber he wanted.
And Solomon gave Hiram twenty thousand cors of wheat as food for his household, and twenty cors of pure oil; thus Solomon gave to Hiram year by year.
The LORD gave Solomon wisdom as he had promised him, and there was peace between Hiram and Solomon, and the two of them made a treaty.
King Solomon conscripted a labor force out of all Israel, and the levy was thirty thousand men.
He sent them to Lebanon in shifts of ten thousand a month; one month they were in Lebanon and two months at home; and Adoniram was over the levy.
Solomon had seventy thousand burden-bearers and eighty thousand stonecutters in the mountains,
besides Solomon's three thousand three hundred chief officers over the work, who supervised the workers.
The king commanded, and they quarried great, costly stones to lay the foundation of the house with dressed stones.
Solomon's builders and Hiram's builders and the Gebalites cut and prepared the timber and stones to build the house.
